etymology.description: semantic 米(米) (mǐ, "rice") + phonetic 且(且) (qiě) -> coarse; rough
etymology.interpretation: The character 粗(cū, "coarse; rough") combines semantic 米(米) (mǐ, "rice"), suggesting something granular or coarse, with phonetic 且(且) (qiě), which supplies the sound. This links to the notion of coarse grains or texture, which is the primary meaning of the character.

examples.chinese: 这条毛巾很粗(這條毛巾很粗)。
他的声音粗粗的(他的聲音粗粗的)，让人感到不舒服(讓人感到不舒服)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de shēngyīn cūcū de, ràng rén gǎndào bù shūfú.
examples.english: His voice is rough, making people feel uncomfortable.

examples.chinese: 这块石头表面很粗糙(這塊石頭表面很粗糙)，要小心触摸(要小心觸摸)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè kuài shítou biǎomiàn hěn cūcāo, yào xiǎoxīn chùmō.
examples.english: The surface of this stone is very rough, touch it carefully.
